heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: The last storm was on 18 July 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 2nd since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 19 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwave right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (206°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 8.0°C) and outside temperature (down 6.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 19%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 2527 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1761 m) and outside humidity (down 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2530m lower than yesterday, 1760.6m lower than the past week, 1046.2m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 1974m and a peak of 8460m, averaging 2660m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, low humidity will be noticeable and near calm conditions. Drizzle or light rain possible, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and it’s dim outside. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; there will not be a great deal of drying overnight.
